title: First Year Puppy Costs 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Puppy First-Year Costs: What to Budget
description: Calculate total first-year puppy expenses including vaccinations, food, vet care, spaying/neutering, and supplies. Get breed-specific estimates.
seoKeywords: how much does a puppy cost
intro: Your puppy's **first year is the most expensive**: multiple vaccines, spaying/neutering, initial setup (bed, collar, bowls, toys), puppy-specific food, and frequent vet checkups. In Argentina, raising a puppy during its first year costs between **ARS 600,000 and ARS 2,500,000** depending on breed and care quality. This calculator adds up all initial expenses plus monthly costs × 12 to show your total first-year investment.
keyTakeaway: **Small breeds**: ARS 600,000–1,000,000. **Medium breeds**: ARS 900,000–1,500,000. **Large breeds**: ARS 1,400,000–2,500,000. Spaying/neutering and initial setup typically account for 25–35% of total costs.
useCases: You're thinking about getting a puppy and want to budget beforehand.
You just adopted a puppy and want to know how much to set aside for year one.
You're comparing puppy costs across different breeds.
You're planning your family's annual budget.

example.title: Example: Beagle with spaying and premium food

explanation:
## Typical First-Year Breakdown

| Item | Cost (ARS) |
|---|---|
| Initial setup (bed, leash, bowls, carrier) | ARS 40,000–100,000 |
| Complete vaccination plan (6–8 doses) | ARS 50,000–100,000 |
| Deworming treatments (every 3 months) | ARS 30,000 |
| Spaying/neutering | ARS 80,000–200,000 |
| Puppy food 12 months | ARS 400,000–1,200,000 |
| Veterinary checkups (4–6 visits) | ARS 60,000 |
| Toys and extras | ARS 40,000 |
| Grooming (shampoo, professional grooming if needed) | ARS 50,000–150,000 |
| Basic training (optional) | ARS 100,000 |

## Costs by Breed Size

Large breeds cost 2–3 times more due to higher food consumption and higher surgical costs. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs) may require additional corrective surgeries.

## How to Save Money

- **Adopt from a shelter**: often includes spaying/neutering, vaccines, and microchip (ARS 0–50,000).
- **Buy food in bulk** (15 kg bags, typically 20% cheaper).
- **Take advantage of vaccination packages** offered by vets (bundled pricing).
- **Spay/neuter early** (6–12 months) to prevent future hormonal complications.

## Emergency Veterinary Costs

Reserve an additional **ARS 300,000** for emergencies (poisonings, injuries). Pet insurance typically costs ARS 5,000–12,000/month and covers up to 80% of veterinary bills.

## Related Calculators

- [Monthly Cost by Breed](/calculadora-costo-mensual-raza-perro)

faq.q: How much does it cost to raise a puppy in Argentina?
faq.a: Between **ARS 600,000 and ARS 2,500,000** in the first year, depending on breed size and care quality. After year one, costs drop about 40% since you won't need initial setup or spaying/neutering.

faq.q: Is premium puppy food worth the extra cost?
faq.a: Yes, especially in the first year. Premium puppy formulas have higher-quality animal protein, essential vitamins, and minerals needed for proper bone and muscle development.

faq.q: When should I spay or neuter my puppy?
faq.a: Between **6 and 12 months**. Small breeds can be done as early as 5 months; large breeds should wait until 12 months to avoid affecting joint development.
Yes. Puppies need a **DHPP vaccine at 6, 8, and 12 weeks**, rabies vaccine at 4 months, and annual boosters. Without vaccines, puppies can die from parvovirus or distemper.

faq.q: Do I need professional puppy training?
faq.a: Basic training at home is essential (house-training, basic commands, bite inhibition). Professional trainers charge around ARS 100,000 for 5 sessions, but many owners successfully train at home using online resources.

faq.q: How much does a veterinary emergency cost?
faq.a: Emergency vet visit: ARS 30,000–50,000. 24-hour hospitalization: ARS 100,000–200,000. Surgery: ARS 200,000–600,000. Pet insurance helps cover these unexpected costs.

faq.q: What's the difference in costs between small, medium, and large breed puppies?
faq.a: Large breeds eat 3–4 times more food and require more expensive surgeries, making first-year costs 2–3 times higher than small breeds. Giant breeds can exceed ARS 2.5 million.

faq.q: Can I reduce costs by skipping vaccines or spaying?
faq.a: No. Vaccines are critical for survival; skipping them risks fatal diseases like parvovirus. Spaying/neutering prevents health problems, behavioral issues, and expensive treatments later.
